article_title: Kyoto Experiment Finds Shear Strain Has No Effect on Strontium Ruthenate Superconductivity, Deepening a 30-Year Mystery

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: Kyoto Experiment Finds Shear Strain Has No Effect on Strontium Ruthenate Superconductivity, Deepening a 30-Year Mystery
content_preview.summary: A Kyoto University team applied three types of shear strain to ultra-thin crystals of the unconventional superconductor Sr2RuO4 and found virtually no change in its transition temperature, ruling out several leading theoretical models and opening new questions about the material's pairing symmetry.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Researchers at Kyoto University have found that shear strain has virtually no effect on the superconducting transition temperature of strontium ruthenate (Sr2RuO4), a result that eliminates several prominent theoretical explanations for the material's behavior and deepens one of the longest-running puzzles in condensed matter physics. The study, published in [Nature Communications](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-025-67307-1), represents one of the most direct experimental te...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent science reporting with clear structure. Complex physics concepts explained accessibly without oversimplification. Good separation of experimental findings, theoretical implications, and open questions.
editor_notes.source_verification: ScienceDaily (fetched): Confirms all key facts — 1994 discovery, Mattoni/Toyota Riken-Kyoto, <10 millikelvin threshold, two-component models ruled out, ultrasound contradiction, UPt3 application. Phys.org (fetched): Confirms Mattoni quote verbatim, three shear strain types, 30 Kelvin temperature, optical imaging, Nature Communications publication. Nature Communications (303 redirect): Could not fetch directly, but both secondary sources confirm the paper's findings and DOI.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. Specific numbers (<10 mK/% strain, 30 K, 1994 discovery year), researcher names, institutional affiliations, and the Mattoni quote all confirmed by multiple sources. The theoretical framing (two-component vs one-component) accurately reflects source reporting.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and measured. Appropriately conveys scientific significance without overstating implications. Good use of 'virtually no effect' rather than absolute claims.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of Sr2RuO4 or this research. Unique niche science topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality science news submission with accurate sourcing. Ready for publication.
